# Epics Next

## Find a better dividend source

Yahoo Finance only provides dividend information to 3 decimal places but most of the dividend information I need is 4 decimal places long or longer. We need to find a better source for dividend information that is still free to use but provides more accurate dividend information.

In my recent searching, it looks like we could scrape the data from either dividendhistory.org or dividendhistory.net.

You should evaluate both sources and determine first if either will work and which is the better choice. If neither will work for our purposes, then you should search for another source that provides more accurate dividend information and is free to use.

Once we have identified a new source for dividend information, we should implement the necessary code to fetch the dividend information from that source and update our process on the server to use the new source instead of Yahoo Finance.

## Get price and dividend on add

When we add a symbol manually or via the CUSIP resolution, we should fetch the current price and dividend information for that symbol and store it like we do when we update fields in the universe screen. This will ensure that we have the most up-to-date information for the symbol as soon as it is added to the system.

## Sorting Is Not Working Correctly

### Multi Column Sorting on Universe Screen

On the Universe Screen, we implemented multi-column sorting in a previous story. The primary sort works but the secondary sort has no impact.

Add e2e tests to verify that multi-column sorting is working correctly on the Universe Screen. This will help ensure that the sorting functionality is working as expected and will prevent regressions in the future.

Then, fix the issue with multi-column sorting on the Universe screen and use the e2e tests to verify that the issue has been resolved.

### Sorting does not work on Account tables.

When I click a column header on the Account tables (Open Positions, Closed Positions, Dividends Deposits), the sort icon appears but the data is not sorted. This is a bug that needs to be fixed.

Make sure you have e2e tests in place to verify that sorting is working correctly on the Account tables and use this to verify that the issue has been resolved once you have implemented the fix.

### Sort/Filter state reflected in the UI on Refresh

While sort/filter state is reflect in the UI for the Universe screen. I'm not sure if it is reflected in the UI for the Account screens.

Make sure we have e2e tests in place to verify that the sort/filter state is reflected in the UI on refresh for both the Universe screen and the Account screens (Open Positions, Closed Positions, Dividends Deposits). If these reveal an issue, you should fix it.

## Empty Date Editor too small

When the date editor is empty, it is not as wide as when it is filled with a date. This makes it difficult to click on the date editor when it is empty. We should make the date editor the same width whether it is empty or filled with a date to improve usability.

Add appropriate tests to verify that the date editor is functioning correctly and that the width is consistent regardless of whether it is empty or filled with a date.

## Lazy Loading

Lazy loading of data is not working as expected although there are signs that it is close.

For all the tables, when the table renders, ALL of the data is loaded and rendered. It should only render the data within the "visible" area of the table +/- the buffer rows. I suspect the main problem is that the code is creating an array of ALL the data at the top level as it renders instead of only retrieving the data for the visible area of the table.

If you need to understand more, be sure to use the Context7 MCP server to lookup how to implement lazy loading of data using SmartSignals from SmartNgRX.

You should also implement e2e tests to verify that lazy loading is working correctly. You should be able to use the browser's developer tools to verify the traffic between the client and the server to ensure that only the data for the visible area of the table is being loaded and rendered as the user scrolls through the table


## Look for code duplication and refactor

Evaluate the codebase for any instances of code duplication and refactor to improve maintainability and reduce technical debt. This includes identifying any repeated logic, similar functions, or components that can be abstracted into reusable modules or services. Refactoring should be done carefully to ensure that existing functionality is not broken and that all tests continue to pass.

- There is at least one known instance of code duplication that jscpd will find for you but this is not the only method you should use to find duplication.

- I would expect the Global Summary screen and the Account Summary screen could be combined

This is different from the jscpd story because the jscpd story only looks for duplicate code which does not necessarily find duplicate functionality. This story is about finding duplicate functionality which may or may not be implemented with duplicate code. You should use your judgement to determine if there is duplicate functionality that can be refactored even if the code is not duplicated.
